text: 12g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.
3g of carbs. sugar and glycemic index matter most here. for a dairy food, that is a strong sign.
11.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.
vitamin b12 at 13% daily value, vitamin a at 10% daily value stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
calcium at 21% daily value stands out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

the upsides first: easy to fit into familiar meals because that lowers friction for regular use. clear calcium identity because that is a core category strength. can be a very convenient protein vehicle because convenience is one of dairy’s strongest practical advantages.

on the weaker side: not everyone tolerates dairy well because that is a real use-case limitation. processing changes the category a lot because the label alone does not tell the whole story. fat quality varies wildly across dairy because some dairy is clean, some really is not.
B tier. Best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
